name: AA Affordable Transportation
owner_or_parent: independent (C. Williams listed resident agent; Carol Williams owner per source list)
districts_or_customers: Baltimore City Public Schools (contract TERMINATED Nov 21, 2016)
recent_news: Nov 2016: Baltimore City Schools terminated AA Affordable's contract after a fatal Nov 2016 crash that killed 6 (driver Chappell had unauthorized status and seizure history); 7 routes reassigned. BBB listing still active; BaltimoreDirect listing shows business hours as 'Closed' all week, suggesting it may be dormant/inactive. No evidence of restart of school-bus operations.
fit_notes: AVOID. Severe safety/reputational baggage (fatal crash, terminated district contract). Likely no current school-bus operations. Not a credible Summit tuck-in candidate; included for completeness/exclusion.

name: Jacoby
districts_or_customers: Adams County school districts (Gettysburg Area SD, Upper Adams SD region)
recent_news: Multi-generational family business (founded ~1964 by Donald J. and Bonnie Lee Jacoby); sources call it 2nd-generation in some places and 4th-generation in others, suggesting active family succession. Recently opened Jacoby's Auto Center as adjacent business. FMCSA Active, 41 power units / 33 drivers, MC-822678, no out-of-service order.
fit_notes: Legitimate, independent, multi-generational family operator with ~41-bus fleet, long-tenured Adams County / Lincoln IU#12 contracts, and a parallel auto-center business that signals owner diversification (possible succession lever). Source data understates scale (rev_est $103K and 3 employees are clearly wrong vs. 33 drivers/41 buses). Solid Summit tuck-in candidate in PA cluster - worth direct outreach despite low source rank.
